title: Associate Director, Grants & Partnerships - Education
description:
• High-exposure stakeholder role.
  • Meaningful impact at national level.

About Our Client

Our client is a leading education institution driving Singapore's lifelong learning and workforce transformation agenda through large-scale, government-funded continuing education programmes in partnership with industry and public sector stakeholders.

Job Description

  • Lead the end-to-end management of government-funded education and training grants, ensuring strong governance, compliance, and audit readiness.
  • Build and manage partnerships with key government agencies and internal stakeholders to support funding applications, reporting, and programme delivery.
  • Provide strategic oversight of grant frameworks, policies, and processes, driving continuous improvement and operational excellence.
  • Manage and develop a team responsible for grant operations, quality assurance, and stakeholder coordination.
  • Prepare high-level reports, insights, and recommendations for senior leadership to support decision-making and long-term funding strategy.

The Successful Applicant

  • A seasoned professional with strong experience in grant management, public sector funding, compliance, or related governance-focused environments.
  • Proven ability to engage senior stakeholders across government, academia, or large organisations, with confidence in navigating regulatory and audit requirements.
  • Demonstrated leadership experience, with the ability to build high-performing teams and drive process improvement.
  • Highly structured, detail-oriented, and comfortable operating in complex, regulated environments.
  • Excellent communication skills, with the maturity and gravitas to operate at senior management level.

What's on Offer

  • A senior leadership role within a highly respected, globally recognised institution with strong stability and long-term career prospects.
  • The opportunity to work on high-impact, nationally significant programmes with close exposure to government agencies and senior stakeholders.
  • A collaborative and purpose-driven environment that values governance excellence, professional development, and continuous improvement.

Contact
